title: sorbus cretica f. cuneifolia
intro: Sorbus cretica f. cuneifolia, also known as a mountain ash variant, is a shrub or small tree native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its distinctive cuneate leaves and vibrant white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Sorbus cretica f. cuneifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve gut health, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as creams or ointments.
